title: 10 best herbal teas for laryngitis

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas Coneflower's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as saponins and flavonoids, help reduce swelling and pain in the throat, providing relief from laryngitis symptoms.

Antiviral properties: The tea's antiviral compounds, like isothiocyanates, inhibit the replication of viral pathogens that cause laryngitis, promoting recovery and reducing the risk of secondary infections.
Mucolytic properties: Kansas Coneflower's mucolytic properties help break down mucus in the throat, making it easier to expel, and reducing congestion and discomfort associated with laryngitis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, teas helps with laryngitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to boost the immune system. The tea's active compounds, such as alkylamides and glycosides, help reduce swelling and congestion in the throat, making it easier to swallow and speak. Additionally, Echinacea angustifolia's immunomodulatory effects stimulate the production of white blood cells, which helps fight off the underlying infection causing laryngitis, promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the throat and vocal cords, alleviating laryngitis symptoms.

Alkaloids (specifically Cichoric Acid and Alkylamides): These compounds exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which help combat infections and reduce oxidative stress in the throat, promoting healing and recovery from laryngitis.

Phenylethanoid Glycosides: These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the throat, alleviating laryngitis symptoms and promoting overall health and well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: Echinacea angustifolia roots are used to make teas for laryngitis because they are rich in alkaloids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Echinacea angustifolia leaves are used to make teas for laryngitis because they contain antioxidants and other compounds that help soothe and protect the throat.

Flowers: Echinacea angustifolia flowers are used to make teas for laryngitis because they have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce swelling and combat infections in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of dried echinacea angustifolia roots with a sharp knife in late fall or early spring.
Grind 1 teaspoon of the harvested roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Steep the ground roots in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-10 minutes in a heat-resistant cup.
Strain the tea mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup.
Enjoy the herbal echinacea angustifolia tea immediately or store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help combat bacterial and viral infections that cause laryngitis by inhibiting the growth of pathogens.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce inflammation and swelling in the throat, relieving discomfort and pain associated with laryngitis.

Antitussive properties: Thyme's expectorant and antispasmodic properties help soothe a sore throat, calm coughing fits, and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing the risk of complications.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, teas helps with laryngitis because of its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oils present in thyme, particularly thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to combat infections and reduce inflammation in the throat. This makes thyme tea an effective remedy for soothing irritated mucous membranes and promoting healing of the vocal cords. Additionally, thyme's decongestant properties help to clear mucus from the airways, providing relief from the discomfort associated with laryngitis.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections causing laryngitis, promoting a reduction in inflammation and soreness.
Caryophyllene: Caryophyllene exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing swelling and discomfort in the throat, alleviating symptoms of laryngitis.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing irritation and inflammation associated with laryngitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are commonly used to make teas due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation associated with laryngitis.
Flowers: Thyme flowers are used in teas to help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the throat, providing relief from symptoms of laryngitis.
Buds: Thyme buds are used in teas to add their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help combat infections and reduce discomfort associated with laryngitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in the morning when the dew is still present on the plant.
Rinse the harvested leaves and flowers thoroughly with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the leaves and flowers into small pieces to increase their surface area and release their oils.
Steep one teaspoon of chopped thymus vulgaris in one cup of boiling water for five to ten minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids before serving it hot or chilled.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce swelling and pain in the throat, providing relief from laryngitis symptoms.

Saponins: Saponins, a type of triterpenoid, help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with laryngitis.

Licoricidin: A phenolic compound found in Glycyrrhiza glabra, licoricidin has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe the mucous membranes and reduce the severity of laryngitis.

Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, saponins, and glycosides that can help reduce inflammation, soothe the throat, and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots for making herbal tea.
Chop the roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Combine 2 teaspoons of chopped roots with 250 ml of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5 minutes to allow the roots to release their active compounds.
Strain the tea and discard the solids to obtain a clear liquid for consumption.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and flowers in late morning for optimal flavor and aroma.
Dry the harvested verbena officinalis leaves and flowers in a single layer for 7-10 days.
Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried verbena officinalis in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids to prevent any bitterness from affecting the flavor.
Enjoy the verbena officinalis tea immediately and store any leftovers in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial and antiviral properties: The essential oils in curry plant tea have been shown to exhibit antibacterial and antiviral activity against pathogens that can cause laryngitis, helping to combat infection and reduce symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Helichrysum italicum, also known as curry plant, teas helps with laryngitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's essential oils, particularly neryl acetate and italidione, have been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe the mucous membranes in the throat. This can help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with laryngitis, making it easier to swallow and speak. remedy_recipe: Harvest the dried helichrysum italicum flowers by cutting the stems near the base of the plant with pruning shears.
Dry the freshly cut flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried helichrysum italicum flowers with 8 ounces of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes to allow the flavors and oils to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool to a comfortable drinking temperature before serving.

Combine one teaspoon of dried fennel leaves or two teaspoons of crushed fennel seeds with boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the volatile oils to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Drink the warm tea immediately, ideally three times a day, with each serving lasting about 10 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried ginger root and three cups of boiling water for infusion.
Measure one tablespoon of grated fresh ginger and steep it in two cups of boiling water for ten minutes.
Combine the infused or steeped ginger tea with honey to taste and a squeeze of lemon juice for added flavor.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean cup before serving immediately.
Consume one cup of the zingiber officinale tea twice daily, morning and evening, for seven to ten days.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to laryngitis that are treated with herbal teas are respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and chronic coughs. Herbs like thyme, peppermint, and eucalyptus are commonly used in tea blends to help soothe irritated throats, reduce inflammation, and loosen phlegm. Additionally, teas made from slippery elm, licorice root, and marshmallow root may also provide relief for sore throats and coughs associated with these conditions.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for laryngitis are a natural remedy that helps alleviate symptoms of this inflammation of the larynx, such as hoarseness, coughing, and difficulty swallowing. These teas are made from various herbs that possess anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them effective in treating laryngitis. The best herbal teas for laryngitis include Glycyrrhiza glabra, Echinacea purpurea, Thymus vulgaris, Zingiber officinale, Eucalyptus globulus, Sambucus nigra, Lavandula angustifolia, Rosmarinus officinalis, Mentha x piperita, and Verbascum thapsus. If left untreated, laryngitis can significantly impact daily life, causing fatigue, loss of productivity, and strained relationships due to persistent coughing and hoarseness.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with laryngitis is a soothing blend of Echinacea, Licorice root, and Thyme. Echinacea is known for its immune-boosting properties, Licorice root helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the throat, while Thyme provides antibacterial properties to combat infection. Drinking a warm tea made from these herbs can help to alleviate symptoms and promote healing. Additionally, Slippery elm and Ginger can also be added for extra comfort and relief.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of alternative and complementary medicine (New York, N.Y.), Throat Coat, a traditional demulcent herbal tea, was investigated in a multicenter, prospective, randomized, double-blinded, placebo-controlled, two-armed, parallel-group clinical trial involving 60 patients with clinical diagnoses of acute pharyngitis. The results found that compared to a placebo, the intensity of throat pain when swallowing was significantly reduced by Throat Coat, with significant differences in change from baseline pain observed at 5, 10, 15, 20, and 30 minutes after the first dose. These findings suggest that herbal teas like Throat Coat can provide rapid and temporary relief from sore throat pain, making them a good option for individuals suffering from laryngitis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas helps with laryngitis because its anti-inflammatory properties soothe the inflamed tissues in the throat, reducing discomfort and pain. Additionally, the tea's antimicrobial properties help to combat infections, promoting a speedy recovery from laryngitis. As a result, Glycyrrhiza glabra tea has been a trusted remedy for centuries to help alleviate the symptoms of laryngitis and promote a speedy recovery.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the mucous membranes in the throat, thereby alleviating symptoms of laryngitis.
Liquiritigenin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to protect the throat from further irritation and promote healing.

Isoliquiritigenin: Displays antimicrobial properties that help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to laryngitis, and its anti-inflammatory effects also help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the throat.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part of Glycyrrhiza glabra for making teas, as they contain high levels of glycyrrhizin, a compound that soothes and reduces inflammation in the throat.
Leaves: The leaves are also used to make teas for laryngitis, as they have antiviral and antibacterial properties that help combat infections.
Roots: Some teas for laryngitis may include roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra in addition to other herbs, as the roots have been traditionally used to calm irritated throats and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra and store them in a cool dry place.
Combine 1 teaspoon of the dried roots in a tea infuser with 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the roots to release their active compounds.
Strain the tea and discard the solids to prevent any potential bitterness.
Drink the tea immediately or store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
